category_description: Automation reject codes: rule scope, actor permissions, approval racing, and stale target references.

fixtures.title: Rule scope mismatch
fixtures.trigger_condition: An automation rule scoped to 'equity-desk' fires on an event generated by a 'rates-desk' actor, which is outside the rule's scope definition.

expected_reject.message: Rule RULE-AUTO-042 scope 'equity-desk' does not include firing actor 'rates-desk-trader-99'. Rule not applied.

fixtures.remediation: Update the rule's scope to include the relevant desk/actor, or create a new rule scoped to 'rates-desk'.

fixtures.title: Action not permitted for actor
fixtures.trigger_condition: Automation rule RULE-AUTO-043 attempts to execute a 'suspend-order' action, but the rule-binder user SYSTEM-AUTO-01 lacks the #order-suspend permission tag.

expected_reject.message: Action 'suspend-order' on rule RULE-AUTO-043 requires tag #order-suspend; SYSTEM-AUTO-01 does not hold it.

fixtures.remediation: Grant #order-suspend to SYSTEM-AUTO-01 via the tag admin, or change the rule action to one the binder is permitted to execute.

fixtures.title: Already approved
fixtures.trigger_condition: Two approvers (USER-A and USER-B) both submit approval for the same automation rule action concurrently; USER-B's approval arrives 50ms after USER-A's which has already taken effect.

expected_reject.message: Action ACT-INST-5512 already approved and executed by USER-A at 14:30:00.100Z; USER-B's approval is a no-op.

fixtures.remediation: No action required — the automation action was successfully applied by the first approver. Review the approval workflow to prevent duplicate approvals.

fixtures.title: Rule references unknown target
fixtures.trigger_condition: An automation rule attempts to cancel order ORD-99999, but that order_id no longer exists in the OMS (it was purged after being filled and archived).

expected_reject.message: Rule RULE-AUTO-045 references unknown target order ORD-99999: not found in OMS (may have been archived).

fixtures.remediation: Verify the target order ID exists and is in an actionable state; update or disable the rule if the target is permanently gone.
